title: Embargoed race-wise CV to detect hidden leakage and retune on the harsher split
summary: The label is a next-lap event, so adjacent rows from the same race/driver can leak future context across folds even when features are shift-based.
rationale: The label is a next-lap event, so adjacent rows from the same race/driver can leak future context across folds even when features are shift-based. Time-series literature recommends gap/embargo style validation when labels depend on future windows, and grouped-data leakage papers show clustered splits can materially inflate CV.
implementation_hint: Replace random/group CV with folds built on ordered `(Year, Race)` blocks, and embargo at least 1-2 laps around each validation block for the same race. Compare current 0.951 CV under standard split vs embargoed split; only trust ideas that survive the harsher estimate.
expected_effect: May lower local CV initially, but should improve leaderboard correlation and reduce overfitting to race-local temporal structure.
risk: Purely a validation change may not raise CV, and if test races are IID with train races, the stricter split can be overly pessimistic.
